description: Generalized medications, allergies, and recent-antibiotic history module. Ask once per session after complaint clerking and ROS unless an urgent complaint-specific safety question already captured the same concept earlier.
ui_render_rule: Use ui_label or text in the UI. Never show raw backend field ids.
module_ownership_principle: Generalized medications, allergies, and recent-antibiotic history are captured here first. Complaint files may ask only targeted medication or treatment follow-up when clinically necessary.
activation_rules: Run once per session after complaint clerking and ROS unless an emergent safety path requires earlier targeted history.
If current_medications, allergies, or recent_antibiotics was already captured earlier in the session, skip the matching question here.
Ask recent_antibiotics only when INFECTIOUS_OR_GU_PRESENTATION is true.

questions.text: What medicines, inhalers, injections, supplements, or other treatments are you currently using?
Do you have any medication, food, or other allergies or bad reactions we should know about?
Have you taken any antibiotics in the last few weeks?

followup_guidance.principle: Keep this module generalized. Complaint files should ask targeted medication follow-up only when the complaint, red flags, or earlier answers make that detail decision-relevant.
examples: hematuria may ask about anticoagulant use as targeted follow-up rather than expanding this module
palpitations may ask about stimulant or thyroid medication use as targeted follow-up
vomiting or diarrhea may ask about recent antibiotic timing if C. difficile or drug-related illness is being considered
